message:
feat(abi): port 8 glibc reserved-namespace resource/timer aliases (bd-1wuit)

Adds the 8 glibc reserved-namespace aliases for resource and
timer entry points across resource_abi, unistd_abi, and
glibc_internal_abi:

- resource_abi: __setrlimit
- unistd_abi: __getrusage, __pathconf, __getitimer, __setitimer,
  __getpriority, __setpriority
- glibc_internal_abi: __times

glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the
symbols statically-linked NSS modules + libpthread internals
link against. Without them callers see undefined-reference
errors when linking statically.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public counterparts — same pattern as the
previously-shipped __pthread_*, __wait*, signal, socket,
poll/epoll, fs path, and IO alias batches.

Tests: 6 abi integration tests covering: __setrlimit to current
RLIMIT_NOFILE succeeds; __getrusage(RUSAGE_SELF) returns 0;
__pathconf(/tmp, _PC_NAME_MAX) returns >= 0; __getitimer +
__setitimer round-trip on ITIMER_REAL with zero values;
__getpriority + __setpriority round-trip on self with PRIO_PROCESS;
__times returns >= 0 clock ticks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1uc","title":"bd-z84 subtask: ABI routing/callthrough eradication guard for pthread_mutex_* replacement paths","description":"Background:
- “No callthrough” claim depends on concrete ABI routing and replacement profile enforcement, not implementation alone.

Goal:
- Eliminate any remaining pthread mutex callthrough usage in replacement-relevant paths and enforce via automated guard.

Deliverables:
1) ABI routing audit for pthread_mutex_* symbols.
2) Replacement profile guard updates to fail on forbidden callthroughs.
3) Support matrix semantics update for affected symbols.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Replacement guard passes with zero forbidden mutex callthroughs.
- Any future reintroduction fails CI deterministically.

Verification & Logging:
- Add routing guard tests and fixture checks.
- Emit structured logs for guard evaluation: trace_id, symbol, status, reason, artifact_ref.","notes":"Closed with scoped mutex replacement-guard deliverables:\n\n1) ABI routing/callthrough audit:\n- Extended guard scanner to detect both libc:: and host_pthread_* call-through patterns.\n- Added structured JSONL + report artifacts:\n  - target/conformance/replacement_guard.log.jsonl\n  - target/conformance/replacement_guard.report.json\n\n2) Replacement profile guard updates:\n- Added explicit forbidden mutex symbol set under replacement_forbidden.mutex_symbols in tests/conformance/replacement_profile.json.\n- scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh now emits deterministic FAIL when pthread_mutex_* call-through appears (even if broader replacement profile remains incomplete).\n- Added harness regression assertions in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/replacement_guard_test.rs:\n  - no_pthread_mutex_call_throughs_in_replacement_paths\n  - replacement_profile_has_both_modes validates mutex symbol manifest.\n\n3) Support matrix semantics updates:\n- support_matrix.json now marks pthread_mutex_{init,destroy,lock,trylock,unlock} as Implemented with futex-managed semantics and deterministic unsupported-layout behavior.\n\nVerification:\n- cargo fmt --all\n- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test replacement_guard_test -- --nocapture (8 passed)\n- bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh interpose (PASS; pthread_mutex_* forbidden count = 0)\n- bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh replacement (expected global FAIL outside mutex scope; pthread_mutex_* forbidden count remains 0).\n\nNote: existing environment-level hang behavior in pthread_mutex_core_test persists and appears independent of this scoped guard work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticCastle","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.149996006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:19:44.754947521Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T02:19:44.754847344Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","pthread","syscall","verification"]}

 {"id":"bd-1v7mf","title":"PORT: bsd_getopt (libbsd BSD-flavored getopt wrapper)","description":"libbsd bsd_getopt(nargc, nargv, options) is a thin BSD-flavored wrapper over POSIX getopt that strips a leading "+" or "-" from the optstring before delegating. The leading "+" in glibc enables POSIX-compatible "stop at first non-option" mode (no permutation); our getopt impl already has that behavior unconditionally, so we just need to skip the prefix and forward. Used by libbsd consumers that explicitly want the historical BSD behavior. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:51:18.405787493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:55:46.485639388Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:55:46.483743318Z","close_reason":"shipped: unistd_abi::bsd_getopt thin wrapper over getopt with +/- prefix strip; 6 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1wuit","title":"PORT: __setrlimit + __getrusage + __pathconf + __getitimer + __setitimer + __getpriority + __setpriority + __times (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the 8 glibc reserved-namespace aliases for resource and timer entry points: __setrlimit, __getrusage, __pathconf, __getitimer, __setitimer, __getpriority, __setpriority, __times. glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols statically-linked NSS modules + libpthread internals link against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public counterparts in resource_abi (setrlimit), unistd_abi (getrusage/pathconf/itimer/priority), and glibc_internal_abi (times). Tests verify each alias produces the same observable behavior as its public counterpart on a representative input.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:54:16.074361314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:54:16.074361314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1wy","title":"Drift: runtime_math module wiring checklist (pub mod/use/fields/cache/snapshot/fusion)","description":"Turn the integration checklist into an explicit gate.

Acceptance criteria:
- A checklist (and optionally a linter/test) that ensures every controller is:
  - declared in mod.rs
  - instantiated in RuntimeMathKernel::new
  - fed in observe_validation_result (if applicable)
  - exported in snapshot
  - optionally included in fusion severity vector

Goal:
- Make it hard to add half-wired kernels.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:14.268835301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:16:38.339402102Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:16:38.339383648Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":699,"issue_id":"bd-1wy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Deliverables:

1. **scripts/check_module_wiring.sh** — Comprehensive wiring checklist script that:
   - Extracts all `pub mod` names from runtime_math/mod.rs
   - Uses explicit field-name mappings (FIELD_MAP) for modules with abbreviated struct field names
   - Checks 5 integration points per module: declared, struct, observe, snapshot, fusion
   - Uses temp-file-based section searching (reliable for 3000+ line files)
   - Supports OBSERVE_EXEMPT list for read-only decision-time guards
   - Reports clear table + summary

2. **Wiring checklist results (45 modules):**
   - 42/45 fully wired (struct + observe + snapshot)
   - 3 gaps found:
     - `cohomology`: MISSING_OBSERVE — by design (monitors shard consistency, not validation results)
     - `coupling`: MISSING_SNAPSHOT — state not yet exported to RuntimeKernelSnapshot
     - `loss_minimizer`: MISSING_SNAPSHOT — state not yet exported to RuntimeKernelSnapshot
   - 1 exempt: `barrier` (read-only admissibility guard)

3. **scripts/ci.sh** — Wired as non-blocking CI gate (warns but doesn't fail)

These 3 gaps should be tracked as follow-up work.","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:16:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1wzq","title":"HTM fast-path for hot libc functions (section 4.2, Score 3.0)","description":"Implement Hardware Transactional Memory (HTM) fast-path for the hottest libc functions on Intel TSX-capable processors. Concept: Use Intel RTM (Restricted Transactional Memory) instructions (XBEGIN/XEND/XABORT) to speculatively execute hot functions without acquiring locks. If the transaction commits, the function executed atomically without any synchronization overhead. If the transaction aborts (due to conflict, capacity, or interrupt), fall back to the standard lock-based path. Target functions: (1) malloc/free — speculative allocation from thread-local cache without arena lock. If cache is sufficient, commit. If not, abort and take the slow path. (2) memcpy for small sizes (<256 bytes) — speculative copy that avoids cache-line locking overhead. (3) pthread_mutex_lock — speculative lock elision (execute the critical section transactionally, skip the lock entirely). Implementation: (a) Runtime CPUID check for RTM support. (b) Adaptive retry logic — track abort rate per call site. If abort rate > threshold (e.g., 30%), disable HTM for that site for a cooldown period. (c) Fallback path must be functionally identical to HTM path. (d) TSM integration — TSM validation must be idempotent (safe to re-execute on transaction retry). Caveat: Intel deprecated TSX on some SKUs. This is a performance opportunity on supported hardware, not a required feature.

**Alien CS Reference:** Section 4.2 of the graveyard. HTM (Intel TSX / IBM POWER HTM) provides speculative lock elision. Key papers: Herlihy & Moss (1993) original HTM proposal; Intel TSX Programming Guide. The graveyard Score 3.0 reflects hardware deprecation risk but high payoff on supported SKUs.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Use core::arch::x86_64::{_xbegin, _xend, _xabort, _xtest} intrinsics behind #[cfg(target_feature = "rtm")].
- Wrap in HtmFastPath<F: Fn() -> R> that encapsulates the speculative-try/fallback pattern.
- Per-call-site abort counter: AtomicU32 with relaxed ordering, sampled every 64 invocations to amortize overhead.
- Cooldown: exponential backoff (1ms, 2ms, 4ms, ... up to 1s) before re-enabling HTM at a disabled site.
- All TSM state reads inside the transaction must be from immutable/const data to avoid transactional capacity aborts.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. HtmFastPath compiles on non-RTM targets as a no-op passthrough (zero overhead).
2. On RTM-capable hardware, malloc fast-path commit rate >70% under low contention (1-4 threads).
3. Abort rate tracking is correct: synthetic abort injection yields accurate per-site counters.
4. Fallback path produces bit-identical results to HTM path for all inputs.
5. Adaptive disable/re-enable logic verified by test that forces >30% abort rate, confirms disable, waits cooldown, confirms re-enable.
6. All operations emit structured tracing: tracing::trace!(target: "htm", site, outcome, abort_reason).
7. Benchmark: malloc/free microbenchmark shows >15% throughput improvement on RTM hardware vs lock-based baseline at 4+ threads.

**Logging Requirements:**
- Every HTM transaction attempt logs: call_site_id, attempt_number, outcome (commit/abort/fallback), abort_code (if abort).
- Periodic (every 1000 ops) summary log: per-site commit rate, abort rate, disabled sites, cooldown remaining.
- All logs use tracing crate with target = "htm" for filterable structured output.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:22.229938007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T17:35:02.269225230Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T17:35:02.268971009Z","close_reason":"Completed HTM fast-path verification slice: added missing pthread_mutex_lock abort/fallback regression so the three integrated HTM entrypoints now have explicit commit/abort coverage (malloc, memcpy, pthread_mutex_lock) on top of the existing site-local cooldown/disable tests in htm_fast_path.rs. Verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi htm -- --nocapture, rch exec cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets, rch exec cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets, rch exec cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo fmt --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1x3","title":"EPIC: Stub Extermination (ranked by real call traces)","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Goal: systematically eliminate todo!/unimplemented! stubs in the surface area required by real programs.

Deliverables:
- Static census of all stubs.
- Dynamic ranking from LD_PRELOAD smoke traces (what real programs call first).
- Replace the top-ranked stubs with real implementations (or explicitly downgrade support classification).

Acceptance:
- No todo!/unimplemented! in the top N (configurable) most-called exported symbols.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"Closed by cc-libc. Owner BrightBrook left the bead at status=open with a generic 'Delivered: code + artifacts present.' close_reason, 1.5 days stale with no owner activity. Independently verified the stub-extermination scope via support_matrix.json: 3980 classified symbols partition into 3576 Implemented + 404 RawSyscall — zero Stub / zero GlibcCallThrough. The epic's named outcome (exterminate stubs ranked by real call traces) is measurably achieved; the underlying data shows no remaining stubs in the classified surface.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BrightBrook","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:06.937118660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:23:22.969896Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:23:22.969896Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:07Z"},{"id":701,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IcyThrush 2026-02-26: fixed stub-census source-of-truth loading in scripts/generate_cross_report_consistency.py. Script now prefers tests/conformance/stub_census.json and falls back to root stub_census.json (legacy), and records report_sources.stub_census for auditability. Validation: python3 -m py_compile scripts/generate_cross_report_consistency.py PASS; python3 scripts/generate_cross_report_consistency.py -o /tmp/cross_report_consistency.test.json PASS (confirmed canonical path loaded). Offloaded verification: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test stub_guard_test -> FAIL due pre-existing baseline issues unrelated to this patch: no_todo_in_abi_crate detects panic! in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs, and implemented_symbols_have_abi_exports flags __progname/__stack_chk_guard/program_invocation_* classification mismatch.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:26:31Z"},{"id":702,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IcyThrush 2026-02-26 follow-up: replaced runtime_policy test tail assertion to avoid both panic! token and clippy constant-assertion ( check). Also extended stub_guard_test exported-static exemptions for __progname, __stack_chk_guard, program_invocation_name, and program_invocation_short_name. Validation attempted via rch offload:  and ; both now fail early on unrelated newly-added ABI files (, ) with unresolved libc symbols/va_list API usage (43 compile errors), blocking final harness gate execution for this bead in current workspace state.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:36:47Z"},{"id":703,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IcyThrush 2026-02-26 follow-up corrected: runtime_policy test now asserts final_decisions > baseline_decisions to avoid panic token and clippy assertions-on-constants lint. Stub guard exemptions extended for exported statics: __progname, __stack_chk_guard, program_invocation_name, program_invocation_short_name. Validation attempts used rch offload with cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test stub_guard_test and cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests. Both are currently blocked by ABI compile failures in fortify_abi.rs and warnings in stdbit_abi.rs introduced outside this patch.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:36:54Z"},{"id":704,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IcyThrush 2026-02-26 validation rerun: rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test stub_guard_test PASS (5 passed, 0 failed). rch exec cargo fmt --check currently FAIL due formatting deltas in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/fortify_abi.rs. rch exec cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings currently FAIL due clippy::unnecessary_cast findings in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdbit_abi.rs. These remaining gate failures are outside bd-1x3-specific edits.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:44:48Z"},{"id":705,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IcyThrush 2026-02-26 unblock pass: aligned ABI regex wrappers in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs to current core API (regex_compile/regex_exec), expanded stub-guard static export exemptions with _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr, and cleaned stdbit unnecessary casts. Validation via rch: cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test stub_guard_test PASS (5 passed, 0 failed). Remaining global gate noise from unrelated files may still affect fmt/clippy workspace-wide runs.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:58:10Z"},{"id":706,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IcyThrush 2026-02-26 current gate snapshot after ABI regex/stub fixes: rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test stub_guard_test PASS (5/5). Only remaining mandatory gate failure is rch exec cargo fmt --check, currently reporting formatting diffs in modified ABI files (pthread_abi.rs, pwd_abi.rs, stdio_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs).","created_at":"2026-02-26T05:02:51Z"},{"id":707,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IcyThrush 2026-02-26 handoff snapshot: completed targeted fixes in runtime_policy.rs, stub_guard_test.rs, generate_cross_report_consistency.py, string_abi.rs, and stdbit_abi.rs. This bead work is merged into current workspace state; gate status currently fluctuates with concurrent edits.","created_at":"2026-02-26T05:17:53Z"},{"id":1410,"issue_id":"bd-1x3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit slice: removed UB from exit/exec child paths by replacing unreachable_unchecked/panic fallthrough with SYS_exit retry loops in process/stdio/stdlib ABI. -{"id":"bd-y1sen","title":"PORT: __pselect / __ppoll / __epoll_wait / __epoll_pwait (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port four glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the poll/epoll family: __pselect, __ppoll, __epoll_wait, __epoll_pwait. glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols statically-linked NSS modules and event-loop libraries link against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public counterparts in poll_abi. Tests: each alias works with a temp pipe / epoll fd (ppoll/pselect with zero-time timeout returns 0; epoll_wait/epoll_pwait with timeout 0 returns 0 on an empty epoll set).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:46:19.002769924Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:46:19.002769924Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-y45u","title":"bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts integration dependency matrix and risk register","description":"Background:
- Hard-parts streams are active but need a single integrated dependency/control matrix to ensure coherent completion.

- Build hard-parts integration matrix spanning startup, NSS, iconv, resolver, locale, and threading dependencies.

1) Cross-subsystem dependency and interface matrix.
2) Integration-risk register with mitigation actions.
3) Milestone sequencing tied to open hard-parts tasks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Matrix is complete enough to drive execution without external plan docs.
- Critical path and parallelizable tracks are explicit.

Verification & Logging:
- Matrix consistency checks.
